description: Defines the minimum evidence requirements for label confidence upgrades, automatic rejection conditions, manual review triggers, and the rules governing unknown whale and medium-to-high label upgrade paths. This policy is binding for v115F/v115G/v115H operator workflows.

minimum_for_high_confidence.description: Minimum evidence requirements that MUST ALL be satisfied before a label can reach high confidence.

requirements.description: At least one primary_source evidence entry with a verifiable trusted source label must be present.
At least one secondary_source evidence entry OR verified cross-source consistency note must be present, providing independent corroboration.
At least one activity_source observation note must be present, documenting on-chain behavior patterns consistent with the claimed identity.
The operator_confirmed_label field in the workbook must be non-empty and must match the claimed entity identity.
The reviewer field must contain a non-empty operator/auditor identifier.
The reviewed_at field must contain a valid ISO-8601 timestamp documenting when the review was completed.
The ready_for_upgrade boolean flag must be explicitly set to true by the operator.
No rejected_source entry must be used as core evidence. Rejected sources may appear as operator notes for awareness but must not be counted toward evidence requirements.
requirements.note: Rejected source presence alone does not block if primary+secondary+activity evidence is complete and verified.

requirements.description: A label at low confidence or marked as unknown whale must NOT be upgraded to high confidence based on a single evidence source alone. Multiple independent sources across primary+secondary+activity categories are required.

minimum_for_medium_confidence.description: Minimum evidence requirements for medium confidence labels. Medium confidence labels MAY retain an operator review but MUST NOT enter TG test group.

requirements.description: Medium confidence labels may retain an operator review for further evidence gathering.
Medium confidence labels MUST NOT be routed to TG test group. tg_test_group_allowed must remain false for any medium-confidence-only label.
Any medium-to-high upgrade MUST pass the complete high_confidence requirements checklist before the upgrade takes effect.
requirements.enforcement: all HC_REQ_001 through HC_REQ_009 must pass

requirements.description: A medium label must NOT be partially upgraded to send-ready. The upgrade must be atomic: all high confidence requirements pass OR the label remains medium.
requirements.enforcement: no partial upgrade; send_allowed remains false until full HC checklist passes

unknown_whale_upgrade_rules.description: Special rules governing addresses labeled as 'Unknown Whale' or with unknown/low confidence. These addresses follow a stricter upgrade path because no entity identity has been established.

rules.description: An unknown whale address MUST first complete manual attribution (establishing which entity it belongs to) before any confidence upgrade can be considered.
rules.enforcement: Block intake if current_label contains 'Unknown' and no operator_confirmed_label is provided.

rules.description: An unknown whale address MUST NOT be directly entered as a send candidate. It must go through the full intake → adjudication → label upgrade pipeline.
rules.enforcement: send_allowed must remain false for unknown whales; upgrade_candidate only after manual attribution complete.

rules.description: A low-confidence unknown whale MUST have at minimum: 1 primary_source + 1 secondary_source + 1 activity_source pattern note + operator confirmation before any upgrade can proceed.
rules.enforcement: All HC_REQ requirements apply; additionally, current_confidence='low' with current_label containing 'Unknown' blocks even medium upgrade until all 4 categories of evidence present.

rules.description: The address must remain blocked (intake_blocked or adjudication_blocked) until all required evidence and manual operator confirmation are complete.
rules.enforcement: intake_ready=false, upgrade_candidate=false, send_allowed=false while any evidence gap remains.

automatic_reject_conditions.description: Conditions under which a label confidence upgrade is automatically rejected without operator discretion.

conditions.description: If ALL evidence provided for a label comes exclusively from rejected_source categories, the intake must be automatically rejected.
If no primary_source evidence is provided, the label cannot reach high confidence and the upgrade path is blocked.
If operator_confirmed_label is empty or operator_confidence_assessment is empty, the intake is automatically blocked.
If the address is an unknown whale and no manual attribution (entity identity) has been established, the intake is automatically blocked.

manual_review_required_conditions.description: Conditions that trigger mandatory manual review but do not automatically reject.

conditions.description: If only one primary source is provided without any secondary corroboration, manual review is required to assess sufficiency.
conditions.action: Flag for operator review; do not auto-upgrade.

conditions.description: If secondary sources exist but no primary source, manual review is required — the operator must locate at least one primary source.
conditions.action: Block until operator adds primary source evidence.

conditions.description: If primary and secondary evidence exist but no activity pattern note has been documented, manual review is required.
conditions.action: Block until operator documents activity pattern observation.

conditions.description: If the operator_confidence_assessment differs from the evidence-based confidence level suggested by the scoring policy, manual review is required.
conditions.action: Flag for reviewer audit; document discrepancy.

medium_to_high_upgrade_rules.description: Rules governing the upgrade path from medium confidence to high confidence.

rules.description: All 9 high_confidence requirements (HC_REQ_001 through HC_REQ_009) must pass before medium can be upgraded to high.
The upgrade must be explicitly reviewed and confirmed by a named operator (reviewer field non-empty, reviewed_at timestamp present).
rules.enforcement: reviewer and reviewed_at must both be non-empty.

rules.description: No partial upgrade from medium to send-ready is permitted. The medium label must first achieve high confidence through the full checklist, and only then may send readiness be evaluated.
rules.enforcement: ready_for_upgrade=true only after all HC_REQ pass; send_allowed evaluated separately by v115D send preview gate.

send_guard_dependency.description: The evidence scoring policy defines what is required for confidence levels; actual TG test group routing and public send are governed by separate policies.
send_guard_dependency.note: Even when high_confidence is achieved, TG test group delivery and public send require separate gate approval per existing v115B routing policy.
